remember: Students will be able to recall the definition of a derivative and its physical significance.
Students will be able to recall the relationship between the derivative of a function and the slope of the tangent to the curve.
Students will be able to recall the formula for finding the derivative of a function.
understand: Students will be able to understand the concept of increasing and decreasing functions using derivatives.
Students will be able to understand the relationship between the sign of the derivative and the concavity of the function.
Students will be able to understand the significance of the second-order derivative in determining the point of inflection.
apply: Students will be able to apply the derivative to find the maximum and minimum values of a function.
Students will be able to apply the derivative to find the intervals where a function is increasing or decreasing.
Students will be able to apply the derivative to find the points of inflection of a function.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the behavior of a function using its derivative and second-order derivative.
Students will be able to analyze the relationship between the derivative and the slope of the tangent to the curve.
Students will be able to analyze the significance of the derivative in optimization problems.

ncert_objectives: To find the derivative of a function and interpret its physical significance.
To apply the derivative to find the maximum and minimum values of a function.
To determine the intervals where a function is increasing or decreasing.
To find the points of inflection of a function.
board_exam_objectives: To find the first and second-order derivatives of a function.
To apply the derivative to solve optimization problems and find the maximum and minimum values of a function.
To analyze the behavior of a function using its derivative and second-order derivative.
